ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Pettler, via ZOOM, for Richard Pettler, Administrator.

Mr. Pettler provides oral testimony regarding the issues he's dealt with regarding this matter and in support of his fee request, including but not limited to, dealing with a beneficiary in South Africa, and dealing with property tax issues. Mr. Pettler points to his supplement that he filed on Nov-18-2021 as being very important to his petition.

After additional discussion, the matter is submited.

Judge: Hon. Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: D. Primus. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (16888 Pine St, E610, San Francisco, Ca 94109)
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Peter Pettler for Administrator.
Andrew De Vries, Listing Agent.

Matter is called for petition to confirm sale of real property at 1688 Pine St., E610, San Francisco, CA 94109. The Court noted that it is waiting for a copy of an order extending sale before it can sign the order confirming sale of property. Mr. Pettler stated the order extending time is for another property and that this property is sold within the time frame.

No one appeared to place an overbid. The sale was confirmed at $730,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court takes the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property under submission. An additional bond in the amount of $730,000.00 is required.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mark A. Miller appearing by CourtCall for Richard J. Pettler.

The Court stated that originally that this matter was pre-granted and that it had concerns with regard to Richard Pettler and Peter Pettler. Mr. Miller stated that Richard Pettler is the cousin of his law partner Peter Pettler, that Richard Pettler was the decedent's best friend, and that Peter Pettler also knows the decendent's mother. Mr. Miller also stated that there were three pieces of property, that the property was owned jointly by the decedent and his mother, that Richard Pettler was asked to do this by the family, and that the beneficiary is out of the country. The Court stated that it was inclined to require bond, give limited authority, and that it would want to see a report of sale. Mr. Miller stated that his client needs to open an estate account. The Court stated that there would be no bond on real property only on income and the cash assets. Mr. Miller argued in support of the Court allowing bond on the full amount and authorizing full authority stating that there is more difficulty with limited authority with regard to the sale of real property. At the conclusion of counsel's argument, the Court took the Petition for Letters of Administration under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.
